{"design":"# Squash Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **\"Squash into Parent...\"** — that opens a dialog supporting both whole-change and selective-file squash. No need for separate subtasks; it's one cohesive feature with a single dialog.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase dialog: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Squash a single mutable change into its parent: \`jj squash -r <rev>\`
- Squash selected files from a change into its parent: \`jj squash -r <rev> <paths...>\`
- Description editing for the combined result
- Option to keep the emptied source change

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- \`--from/--into\` arbitrary source/destination squash (not just into parent)
- Multi-source squash (\`--from A --from B --into C\`)
- Interactive hunk-level selection (\`--interactive\`)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **\"Squash into Parent...\"** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase (line 49 area of \`JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt\`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/edit):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — \`jj squash\` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (\`!entry.immutable\`)
- Enabled for working copy too (squash @ into @-)

**Null-guard pattern**:
\`\`\`kotlin
add(squashAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
\`\`\`

## Dialog Design: SquashDialog

A \`DialogWrapper\` with vertical layout, simpler than RebaseDialog (no destination picker needed since destination is always the parent).

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being squashed — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

#### 2. Destination Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the parent change (destination of squash) — same HTML rendering:
- \"Into parent:\" label
- Change ID + description + decorations

#### 3. Files Panel (expandable, main area)
A \`JujutsuChangesTree\` **with checkboxes enabled** (\`showCheckboxes = true\`).

**File loading**: Call \`jj diff --summary -r <revision>\` to get the file list for the source change, then parse into \`Change\` objects (reuse \`JujutsuChangeProvider.parseStatus\` parsing logic or similar \`diffSummary\` parser).

**Checkbox behavior** (provided by IntelliJ's \`ChangesTree\` infrastructure):
- All files checked by default (= whole squash)
- User unchecks files to exclude them (= selective squash)
- Directory nodes show three-state: all/some/none children selected
- Click on directory checkbox toggles all children
- Space key toggles selected node(s)

**Toolbar**: Expand All / Collapse All buttons (same as existing \`JujutsuChangesTree\` pattern). Group-by-directory toggle.

#### 4. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the squashed result's description.

**Pre-population strategy**: Concatenate source and destination descriptions, separated by a blank line (matching \`jj squash\` default behavior):
\`\`\`
<destination description>

<source description>
\`\`\`

If the source description is empty (\`(no description set)\`), just show the destination description.

#### 5. Options (single row)
- **\"Keep emptied source\"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to \`--keep-emptied\` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **\"Squash\"** button (OK action) — executes the squash
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be selected (disable \"Squash\" button otherwise)
- If all files are selected, omit file paths from command (simpler, whole squash)

### Data Structure
\`\`\`kotlin
data class SquashSpec(
    val revision: Revision,        // Source change to squash
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,  // Empty = all files (whole squash)
    val description: Description?,  // null = let jj merge descriptions; non-null = -m flag
    val keepEmptied: Boolean        // --keep-emptied flag
)
\`\`\`

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (\`CommandExecutor.kt\`)
\`\`\`kotlin
fun squash(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    keepEmptied: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
\`\`\`

### CLI implementation (\`CliExecutor.kt\`)
\`\`\`kotlin
override fun squash(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied) =
    execute(root, squashArgs(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied))

internal fun squashArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    keepEmptied: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add(\"squash\")
    add(\"-r\"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add(\"-m\"); add(description.actual) }
    if (keepEmptied) add(\"--keep-emptied\")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
\`\`\`

## Action Factory (\`squashAction.kt\`)

\`\`\`kotlin
fun squashAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, \"log.action.squash\", <squash-icon>) {
        val repo = target.repo
        // Load file changes for this revision (background, then show dialog on EDT)
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { diffSummary(target.id) }
            .onSuccess { stdout ->
                val changes = parseDiffSummary(stdout, repo)
                val dialog = SquashDialog(project, target, changes)
                if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@onSuccess
                val spec = dialog.result ?: return@onSuccess
                repo.commandExecutor
                    .createCommand { squash(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.keepEmptied) }
                    .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
                    .onFailureTellUser(\"log.action.squash.error\", project, log)
                    .executeAsync()
            }
            .executeAsync()
    }
\`\`\`

## Refresh After Squash

Call \`repo.invalidate(select = ...)\`:
- If \`keepEmptied = false\`: select the parent (since source is gone) — but actually, the change ID persists since it's the destination. Select the destination (parent) change.
- If \`keepEmptied = true\`: select the source change (it's still there, just empty now)

## File Structure

New files:
- \`src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/squashAction.kt\` — Action factory
- \`src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/squash/SquashDialog.kt\` — Dialog

Modified files:
- \`CommandExecutor.kt\` — Add \`squash()\` method
- \`CliExecutor.kt\` — Implement \`squash()\` + \`squashArgs()\`
- \`JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt\` — Add squash to context menu
- \`JujutsuBundle.properties\` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
\`\`\`properties
log.action.squash=Squash into Parent...
log.action.squash.tooltip=Squash this change into its parent
log.action.squash.error.title=Squash Failed
log.action.squash.error.message=Failed to squash:\n{0}
dialog.squash.title=Squash into Parent
dialog.squash.source=Source
dialog.squash.destination=Into parent
dialog.squash.files=Files to squash
dialog.squash.files.none=Select at least one file
dialog.squash.description=Combined description
dialog.squash.keep.emptied=Keep emptied source change
dialog.squash.button=Squash
\`\`\`

## Tests

- \`CliExecutorSquashTest.kt\` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations (whole, selective, with description, keep-emptied)
- \`JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt\` — Add squash availability tests (immutable filtered out, single-select only, mutable allowed)

## Design Rationale

**Single action with file tree vs. two separate actions**: A single dialog with checkboxes (all checked by default) covers both whole and selective squash. Users who want whole squash just click \"Squash\" immediately. Users who want selective squash uncheck files. This matches IntelliJ's commit dialog pattern and avoids polluting the context menu with two similar entries.

**No destination picker**: Unlike rebase, squash destination is always the parent. Showing a destination picker would add complexity for a rare use case (\`--from/--into\`). That can be a separate \"Squash Into...\" action in a future issue.

**Description pre-population**: Concatenating descriptions matches \`jj squash\` default behavior. Users can edit before confirming. This avoids the jarring experience of jj opening \$EDITOR.

**Checkbox tree reuse**: \`JujutsuChangesTree\` already extends \`AsyncChangesTreeImpl.Changes\` which supports checkboxes natively. We just need to pass \`showCheckboxes = true\` (currently \`false\`). The three-state checkbox behavior, directory toggling, and keyboard handling come for free from the platform."}

{"design":"## Problem

`repositoryStates` serves three consumers with different needs:

| Consumer | Need | Sensitivity |
|---|---|---|
| **Log panel** | "Something changed, reload" | Unconditional — any VCS operation |
| **Working copy panel** | Actual `LogEntry` for `@` | Only when WC state changes |
| **VcsDirtyScopeManager** | Which repos' files changed | Only when WC state changes |

The log panel's subscription to `repositoryStates` is a semantic mismatch. It doesn't care about WC state — it just needs "something happened." But `repositoryStates` suppresses notifications when WC hasn't changed (correct for other consumers, wrong for log).

## Solution: `logRefresh` Notifier

Add a `Notifier<Unit>` to `JujutsuStateModel` that fires **unconditionally** after any VCS operation:

```kotlin
val logRefresh = simpleNotifier<Unit>(project, "Jujutsu Log Refresh")
```

**This is a `Notifier`, not a `NotifiableState`** — it has no loader, no equality check, no stored value. It's fire-and-forget, which is exactly what the log panel needs.

### Signal flow after change

```
VCS action → repo.invalidate(select)
  ├── logRefresh.notify(Unit)           ← ALWAYS fires → log panel refreshes
  ├── repositoryStates.invalidate()     ← WC panel + dirty scope (only if WC changed)
  └── changeSelection.notify(key)       ← selection request

File change → BulkFileListener (300ms debounce)
  ├── logRefresh.notify(Unit)           ← log refreshes on file changes too
  └── repositoryStates.invalidate()     ← WC panel + dirty scope (only if WC changed)
```

### Changes required

1. `JujutsuStateModel`: add `val logRefresh = simpleNotifier<Unit>(project, "Jujutsu Log Refresh")`
2. `JujutsuRepository.invalidate()`: add `stateModel.logRefresh.notify(Unit)`
3. `BulkFileListener` debounced block: also fire `logRefresh.notify(Unit)`
4. `UnifiedJujutsuLogPanel.setupStateListener()`: subscribe to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`

### Future evolution

`Notifier<Unit>` can evolve to `Notifier<LogRefreshHint>`:

```kotlin
sealed interface LogRefreshHint {
    data object Full : LogRefreshHint
    data class Revset(val expression: Expression) : LogRefreshHint
}
```

This would allow targeted log reloads (e.g., reload only the subtree of an abandoned change) rather than full reload. But the unconditional `Unit` notifier establishes the right architecture first."}

{"id":"jj-idea-i4k5","title":"Add semantic 'log changed' state to state model","description":"Currently `repositoryStates` is used as a proxy for 'any log change'. A proper 'log changed' state would be semantically correct and allow finer-grained refresh.

**Context**: The log panel listens to `repositoryStates` changes, but this is actually a working copy/repository state signal. When we want to refresh the log specifically (e.g., after operations that don't change working copy status), we don't have a proper semantic signal.

**Proposed solution**:
- Add a `logChanged` notifiable state to JujutsuStateModel
- JujutsuLogPanel subscribes to this instead of repositoryStates
- Operations that affect the log (new, edit, abandon, describe, etc.) invalidate logChanged
- Operations that only affect working copy files invalidate repositoryStates

**Benefits**:
- Cleaner separation of concerns
- Allows finer-grained refresh
- More semantic state model

**Related**: This emerged from jj-idea-850 during the workingCopies → repositoryStates rename.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T14:18:14Z","updated_at":"2026-01-29T14:18:14Z"}

{"design":"## Root Cause

`abandonChangeAction` calls `repo.invalidate(select = WorkingCopy)`, which calls `repositoryStates.invalidate()`. The loader runs `jj log -r @` and compares with the previous value via `StateKey` equality. When abandoning a non-WC change, the working copy hasn't changed, so `NotifiableState` suppresses the notification. The log panel only subscribes to `repositoryStates`, so it never hears about the abandon → no refresh.

This is a symptom of the architectural mismatch described in jj-idea-i4k5: the log panel subscribes to a working-copy-state signal, but needs an unconditional "repo changed" signal.

## Fix

Implement jj-idea-i4k5 (add `logRefresh` notifier). The log panel subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. Since `logRefresh` fires unconditionally from `repo.invalidate()`, the log always refreshes after abandon.

## Selection after abandon

Per comment: currently selects working copy even when abandoning a different change. After implementing this fix, the abandon action should select one of the abandoned change's parents instead of `WorkingCopy`. This requires the action to resolve parent IDs before abandoning (from the `LogEntry.parentIds`)."}

{"design":"## Problem

The log reloads on every file change because it subscribes to `repositoryStates`, which invalidates on a 300ms debounce from BulkFileListener. Editing a file in the IDE triggers a full log reload even though only the working copy entry could have changed.

## Analysis

After jj-idea-i4k5 is implemented, the log subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. This creates two options:

1. **Don't fire `logRefresh` from BulkFileListener at all** — file edits don't change log history, only the WC entry. The WC panel already handles this via `repositoryStates`. The log would only refresh on explicit VCS operations and the refresh button.

2. **Debounce `logRefresh` separately** with a longer interval (e.g., 1-2 seconds) for file-triggered refreshes vs immediate for VCS operations.

Option 1 is simpler and probably correct — the log shows commit history, not live file status. The WC entry in the log (empty/non-empty flag, description) only changes via VCS operations, not file edits.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 must land first to decouple log refresh from `repositoryStates`."}

{"design":"## Context

This bead is about the standard `VcsLogProvider.subscribeToRootRefreshEvents()` hook, which is for the built-in IntelliJ VCS log. Since we use a custom log panel (not IntelliJ's built-in log), this hook is less relevant — it would only matter if we keep the legacy log integration (jj-idea-v1o).

For the custom log, external JJ operations (CLI commands outside the IDE) should trigger a refresh. After jj-idea-i4k5, the `logRefresh` notifier is the right hook point.

## Design

**For external JJ operation detection**: Watch `.jj/repo/op_heads/` directory for changes. When op_heads change, an external operation has occurred. Fire `logRefresh.notify(Unit)` (debounced).

The BulkFileListener in `JujutsuStateModel` currently filters OUT `.jj` directory events via `isUnderJjDirectory()`. To detect external operations, add a separate check for `.jj/repo/op_heads/` changes:

```kotlin
// In BulkFileListener.after()
if (events.any { isOpHeadsChange(it) }) {
    // External JJ operation detected — debounce and refresh log
    logRefreshAlarm.cancelAllRequests()
    logRefreshAlarm.addRequest({ logRefresh.notify(Unit) }, 500)
}
```

**For the legacy VCS log hook** (`subscribeToRootRefreshEvents`): Same approach — watch op_heads and call `refresher.refresh(root)`. But this is only needed if the legacy log is kept.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 provides the `logRefresh` notifier to fire."}

{"design":"## Context

`repositoryStates` recomputes all working copies across all repos when any single repo changes. After jj-idea-i4k5, this concern is orthogonal to the log refresh path — `repositoryStates` only serves the working copy panel and dirty scope.

## Design (unchanged from original)

Key `repositoryStates` by repository so that invalidation is per-repo. Options:

1. Change to `NotifiableState<Map<JujutsuRepository, LogEntry>>` with per-repo invalidation
2. Keep current structure but only reload the changed repo's entry, merging with cached entries for other repos

Option 2 is simpler: the loader receives a hint about which repo changed, loads only that repo's `jj log -r @`, and merges with the cached set.

## Relationship to jj-idea-i4k5

Independent. `logRefresh` fires unconditionally (no per-repo optimization needed for the log signal). `repositoryStates` per-repo optimization only affects the WC panel and dirty scope paths."}

**Context**: The log panel listens to `repositoryStates` changes, but this is actually a working copy/repository state signal. When we want to refresh the log specifically (e.g., after operations that don't change working copy status), we don't have a proper semantic signal.

**Proposed solution**:
- Add a `logChanged` notifiable state to JujutsuStateModel
- JujutsuLogPanel subscribes to this instead of repositoryStates
- Operations that affect the log (new, edit, abandon, describe, etc.) invalidate logChanged
- Operations that only affect working copy files invalidate repositoryStates

- Allows finer-grained refresh

**Related**: This emerged from jj-idea-850 during the workingCopies → repositoryStates rename.","design":"## Problem

`repositoryStates` serves three consumers with different needs:

| Consumer | Need | Sensitivity |
|---|---|---|
| **Log panel** | "Something changed, reload" | Unconditional — any VCS operation |
| **Working copy panel** | Actual `LogEntry` for `@` | Only when WC state changes |
| **VcsDirtyScopeManager** | Which repos' files changed | Only when WC state changes |

The log panel's subscription to `repositoryStates` is a semantic mismatch. It doesn't care about WC state — it just needs "something happened." But `repositoryStates` suppresses notifications when WC hasn't changed (correct for other consumers, wrong for log).

## Solution: `logRefresh` Notifier

Add a `Notifier<Unit>` to `JujutsuStateModel` that fires **unconditionally** after any VCS operation:

```kotlin
val logRefresh = simpleNotifier<Unit>(project, "Jujutsu Log Refresh")
```

**This is a `Notifier`, not a `NotifiableState`** — it has no loader, no equality check, no stored value. It's fire-and-forget, which is exactly what the log panel needs.

### Signal flow after change

```
VCS action → repo.invalidate(select)
  ├── logRefresh.notify(Unit)           ← ALWAYS fires → log panel refreshes
  ├── repositoryStates.invalidate()     ← WC panel + dirty scope (only if WC changed)
  └── changeSelection.notify(key)       ← selection request

File change → BulkFileListener (300ms debounce)
  ├── logRefresh.notify(Unit)           ← log refreshes on file changes too
  └── repositoryStates.invalidate()     ← WC panel + dirty scope (only if WC changed)
```

### Changes required

1. `JujutsuStateModel`: add `val logRefresh = simpleNotifier<Unit>(project, "Jujutsu Log Refresh")`
2. `JujutsuRepository.invalidate()`: add `stateModel.logRefresh.notify(Unit)`
3. `BulkFileListener` debounced block: also fire `logRefresh.notify(Unit)`
4. `UnifiedJujutsuLogPanel.setupStateListener()`: subscribe to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`

### Future evolution

`Notifier<Unit>` can evolve to `Notifier<LogRefreshHint>`:

```kotlin
sealed interface LogRefreshHint {
    data object Full : LogRefreshHint
    data class Revset(val expression: Expression) : LogRefreshHint
}
```

This would allow targeted log reloads (e.g., reload only the subtree of an abandoned change) rather than full reload. But the unconditional `Unit` notifier establishes the right architecture first.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-29T14:18:14Z","updated_at":"2026-03-01T10:46:21Z"}

{"design":"## Design Decision: Context Menu Actions (Approach B)

Add bookmark management via context menus on existing log surfaces, not a standalone popup or panel. Matches JJ CLI power user audience.

### Primary Actions
1. **Create bookmark** on any change
2. **Move bookmark** to selected change, with force-push protection
3. **Track remote bookmark** (e.g., main@origin)

### Secondary Actions
4. Delete (propagates to remotes)
5. Forget (local only)
6. Rename
7. Untrack remote

### Force-Push Protection (Two Levels)
- **Level 1 (JJ built-in)**: Try `jj bookmark set` without `-B`. If JJ refuses (backwards/sideways), offer force-move dialog. JJ's check works on commit IDs, correctly catches rewrites.
- **Level 2 (plugin-level)**: Before moving, compare new target against tracked remote copies' commit IDs. Warn if the move would require a Git force push. Uses `jj bookmark list --all-remotes` for remote commit IDs.

### Key Technical Decisions
- Use `jj bookmark set` (not `move`) for move operations — simpler API, creates-or-updates
- Enhance `BookmarkItem` with `commitId`, `tracked`, `synced` fields
- Add `isRemote`, `localName`, `remote` properties to `Bookmark` class
- Use `logService.getBookmarks()` (jj bookmark list) for move chooser — complete unlike log entries

### Full design: docs/design/jj-idea-qgz-bookmark-management.md"}

{"notes":"## Research Notes

### JJ bookmark set vs move
- `set`: creates OR updates. Simpler. Use this for plugin move operations.
- `move`: only moves existing. Has `--from` for bulk pattern-based moves. Not needed for UI.
- Both have `--allow-backwards` / `-B` flag.

### JJ's --allow-backwards (verified experimentally)
- Checks commit ID ancestry, not change ID ancestry
- Correctly catches: backward, sideways, and rewrite-sideways (same change ID, different commit ID)
- Error: "Refusing to move bookmark backwards or sideways: <name>"

### Template fields for jj bookmark list
- `name`, `remote` (with --all-remotes), `normal_target.change_id()`, `normal_target.commit_id()`
- `tracked` (bool), `synced` (bool), `tracking_present` (bool)
- `tracking_ahead_count` / `tracking_behind_count` (SizeHint, not directly printable as string)

### Bookmark data sources
- LogEntry.bookmarks: only bookmarks on commits in loaded revset range
- logService.getBookmarks() (jj bookmark list): ALL bookmarks, but currently missing commitId
- jj bookmark list --all-remotes: ALL including remotes, with commit IDs via template

### Current code locations
- Bookmark type: jj/Revset.kt (value class, implements Ref)
- BookmarkItem: jj/BookmarkItem.kt (bookmark + changeId)
- Parsing: jj/cli/CliLogService.kt (getBookmarks(), log template)
- Rendering: ui/components/TextCanvas.kt (append(bookmark)), ui/log/JujutsuLogTableRenderers.kt
- Context menu: ui/log/JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt
- Action pattern: vcs/actions/ (see editChangeAction.kt for template)"}

{"design":"# Split Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Split..."** — that opens a dialog letting the user choose which files stay in the original commit and which move to a new child (or sibling) commit.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase and squash dialogs: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Split a single mutable change: `jj split -r <rev>`
- Select files that stay in the first commit (checked = stays, unchecked = moves to new child)
- Description editing for the first commit
- Parallel commits option (`--parallel`)

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)
- Custom description for the second commit (jj prompts for this or uses remainder)

## CLI Reference

`jj split -r <rev> [-m <message>] [--parallel] [<filesets>...]`

- **Filesets** specify what STAYS in the first commit (original position)
- Remaining files move to a new child commit (or sibling with `--parallel`)
- If no filesets given, jj opens an interactive editor (we always pass filesets from the UI)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Split..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase and Squash (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/squash):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj split` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (split @)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SplitDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout.

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being split — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

Uses `IconAwareHtmlPane` + `htmlString {}` (same pattern as `RebaseDialog.createSourcePanel()`).

#### 2. File Selection Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**Checkbox semantics**:
- **Checked** = file stays in the original (first) commit
- **Unchecked** = file moves to the new child commit
- All files checked by default (user unchecks files to move them out)

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`.

#### 3. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the first commit's description.

**Pre-population**: Original description of the source commit.

#### 4. Options (single row)
- **"Create parallel commits"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--parallel` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Split"** button (OK action) — executes the split
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be unchecked (otherwise no split happens — nothing moves to second commit)
- At least one file must be checked (otherwise original becomes empty — warn but allow)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SplitSpec(
    val revision: Revision,
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,   // Files for first commit (checked). Empty = all.
    val description: Description?,    // -m flag for first commit
    val parallel: Boolean             // --parallel flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun split(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    parallel: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun split(revision, filePaths, description, parallel) =
    execute(root, splitArgs(revision, filePaths, description, parallel))

internal fun splitArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    parallel: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("split")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (parallel) add("--parallel")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`splitAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun splitAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.split", AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SplitDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { split(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.parallel) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.split.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

**Note**: Unlike squash where we need to load files async then show dialog, split loads files synchronously because the action already runs from the nullAndDumbAwareAction context (which is on EDT after user clicks). To avoid blocking EDT, wrap file loading in a background task or use the two-phase async pattern from squash.

## Refresh After Split

Call `repo.invalidate(select = target.id)`:
- The original change retains its change ID with the "kept" files
- The new child commit gets a new change ID
- Select the original change so the user can see the result

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/splitAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/split/SplitDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:
- `CommandExecutor.kt` — Add `split()` method
- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `split()` + `splitArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add split to context menu
- `JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
```properties
log.action.split=Split...
log.action.split.tooltip=Split this change into two changes
log.action.split.error.title=Split Failed
log.action.split.error.message=Failed to split change:\n{0}
dialog.split.title=Split Change
dialog.split.source=Source
dialog.split.files=Files to keep in first commit
dialog.split.files.hint=Checked files stay in the original commit; unchecked files move to a new child
dialog.split.description=Description for first commit
dialog.split.parallel=Create parallel commits (siblings instead of parent/child)
dialog.split.validation.none.unchecked=Uncheck at least one file to split
dialog.split.button=Split
```

## Tests

- `CliExecutorSplitTest.kt` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations:
  - Basic split (revision only)
  - With file paths
  - With `-m` description
  - With `--parallel`
  - All combinations

- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt` — Add split availability tests:
  - Immutable entries filtered out (single-select, `!immutable`)
  - Mutable entries pass through
  - Working copy mutable entries pass through
  - Multi-select returns null (single-select only)

## Design Rationale

**Checked = stays, unchecked = moves**: This matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what stays. Users think "I want to keep these files here and move the rest out."

**No description for second commit**: `jj split` handles the second commit's description automatically (uses the original description or prompts). Adding a second text area would complicate the UI for a rare need.

**Parallel option**: Simple checkbox because it's a single flag. Advanced users who want parallel commits can toggle it; most users leave it unchecked.

**Shared FileSelectionPanel**: Reuses the same component as squash, avoiding duplicate checkbox tree infrastructure."}

- Option to keep the emptied source change

**Out of scope (future issues)**:

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout, simpler than RebaseDialog (no destination picker needed since destination is always the parent).

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being squashed — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list for the source change, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`. This handles rename detection and all status types.

- Multi-source squash (`--from A --from B --into C`)
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Squash into Parent..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/edit):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj squash` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (squash @ into @-)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(squashAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SquashDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout, simpler than RebaseDialog (no destination picker needed since destination is always the parent).

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being squashed — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

#### 2. Destination Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the parent change (destination of squash) — same HTML rendering:
- "Into parent:" label
- Change ID + description + decorations

#### 3. Files Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list for the source change, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`. This handles rename detection and all status types.

**Checkbox behavior** (provided by IntelliJ's `ChangesTree` infrastructure via `FileSelectionPanel`):
- All files checked by default (= whole squash)
- User unchecks files to exclude them (= selective squash)
- Directory nodes show three-state: all/some/none children selected
- Click on directory checkbox toggles all children
- Space key toggles selected node(s)

#### 4. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the squashed result's description.

**Pre-population strategy**: Concatenate source and destination descriptions, separated by a blank line (matching `jj squash` default behavior):
```
<destination description>

<source description>
```

If the source description is empty (`(no description set)`), just show the destination description.

#### 5. Options (single row)
- **"Keep emptied source"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--keep-emptied` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Squash"** button (OK action) — executes the squash
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be selected (disable "Squash" button otherwise)
- If all files are selected, omit file paths from command (simpler, whole squash)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SquashSpec(
    val revision: Revision,        // Source change to squash
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,  // Empty = all files (whole squash)
    val description: Description?,  // null = let jj merge descriptions; non-null = -m flag
    val keepEmptied: Boolean        // --keep-emptied flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun squash(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    keepEmptied: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun squash(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied) =
    execute(root, squashArgs(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied))

internal fun squashArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    keepEmptied: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("squash")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (keepEmptied) add("--keep-emptied")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`squashAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun squashAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.squash", <squash-icon>) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SquashDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { squash(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.keepEmptied) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.squash.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

Call `repo.invalidate(select = ...)`:
- If `keepEmptied = false`: select the parent (since source is gone) — but actually, the change ID persists since it's the destination. Select the destination (parent) change.
- If `keepEmptied = true`: select the source change (it's still there, just empty now)

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Split..."** — that opens a dialog letting the user choose which files stay in the original commit and which move to a new child (or sibling) commit.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase and squash dialogs: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Split a single mutable change: `jj split -r <rev>`
- Select files that stay in the first commit (checked = stays, unchecked = moves to new child)
- Description editing for the first commit
- Parallel commits option (`--parallel`)

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)
- Custom description for the second commit (jj prompts for this or uses remainder)

## CLI Reference

`jj split -r <rev> [-m <message>] [--parallel] [<filesets>...]`

- **Filesets** specify what STAYS in the first commit (original position)
- Remaining files move to a new child commit (or sibling with `--parallel`)
- If no filesets given, jj opens an interactive editor (we always pass filesets from the UI)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Split..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase and Squash (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/squash):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj split` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (split @)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SplitDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout.

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being split — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

Uses `IconAwareHtmlPane` + `htmlString {}` (same pattern as `RebaseDialog.createSourcePanel()`).

#### 2. File Selection Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**Checkbox semantics**:
- **Checked** = file stays in the original (first) commit
- **Unchecked** = file moves to the new child commit
- All files checked by default (user unchecks files to move them out)

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`.

#### 3. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the first commit's description.

**Pre-population**: Original description of the source commit.

#### 4. Options (single row)
- **"Create parallel commits"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--parallel` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Split"** button (OK action) — executes the split
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be unchecked (otherwise no split happens — nothing moves to second commit)
- At least one file must be checked (otherwise original becomes empty — warn but allow)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SplitSpec(
    val revision: Revision,
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,   // Files for first commit (checked). Empty = all.
    val description: Description?,    // -m flag for first commit
    val parallel: Boolean             // --parallel flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun split(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    parallel: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun split(revision, filePaths, description, parallel) =
    execute(root, splitArgs(revision, filePaths, description, parallel))

internal fun splitArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    parallel: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("split")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (parallel) add("--parallel")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`splitAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun splitAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.split", AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SplitDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { split(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.parallel) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.split.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

**Note**: Unlike squash where we need to load files async then show dialog, split loads files synchronously because the action already runs from the nullAndDumbAwareAction context (which is on EDT after user clicks). To avoid blocking EDT, wrap file loading in a background task or use the two-phase async pattern from squash.

## Refresh After Split

Call `repo.invalidate(select = target.id)`:
- The original change retains its change ID with the "kept" files
- The new child commit gets a new change ID
- Select the original change so the user can see the result

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/splitAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/split/SplitDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:

- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `split()` + `splitArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add split to context menu

## Design Rationale

**Checked = stays, unchecked = moves**: This matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what stays. Users think "I want to keep these files here and move the rest out."

**No description for second commit**: `jj split` handles the second commit's description automatically (uses the original description or prompts). Adding a second text area would complicate the UI for a rare need.

**Parallel option**: Simple checkbox because it's a single flag. Advanced users who want parallel commits can toggle it; most users leave it unchecked.

{"id":"jj-idea-19g3","title":"(BUG) Squash into Parent does not select parent in log after operation","description":"After squashing the working copy into its parent (with 'keep emptied' unchecked), the log does not select the parent change (which is now the working copy after the squash+edit@- sequence).

## Root cause investigation so far
The squash action calls `invalidate(select = parentEntry.id)`. The pending selection mechanism in JujutsuLogTable was partially fixed: setEntries no longer overwrites explicit pending selections with auto-reselect. However, selection still fails.

Possible causes to investigate:
1. The ChangeKey uses the old repo object which may not match after refresh (object identity vs value equality)
2. The parentEntry.id may not match after squash because jj may rewrite the parent's commit hash (the change ID stays the same but CommitId changes) — need to verify ChangeId.equals handles this
3. The selection notify may fire before the file listener triggers data reload, and then get consumed by stale data
4. The debounced file listener refresh may overwrite the pending selection (setEntries is called multiple times)

## Steps to reproduce
1. Have a working copy with changes and a mutable parent
2. Right-click working copy in log → Squash into Parent
3. Click Squash (with 'keep emptied' unchecked)
4. Expected: parent (now working copy) is selected in log
5. Actual: no entry selected, or wrong entry selected

{"design":"# Split Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Split..."** — that opens a dialog letting the user choose which files stay in the original commit and which move to a new child (or sibling) commit.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase and squash dialogs: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Split a single mutable change: `jj split -r <rev>`
- Select files that stay in the first commit (checked = stays, unchecked = moves to new child)
- Description editing for the first commit
- Parallel commits option (`--parallel`)

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)
- Custom description for the second commit (jj prompts for this or uses remainder)

## CLI Reference

`jj split -r <rev> [-m <message>] [--parallel] [<filesets>...]`

- **Filesets** specify what STAYS in the first commit (original position)
- Remaining files move to a new child commit (or sibling with `--parallel`)
- If no filesets given, jj opens an interactive editor (we always pass filesets from the UI)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Split..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase and Squash (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/squash):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj split` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (split @)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(splitAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SplitDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout.

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being split — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

Uses `IconAwareHtmlPane` + `htmlString {}` (same pattern as `RebaseDialog.createSourcePanel()`).

#### 2. File Selection Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**Checkbox semantics**:
- **Checked** = file stays in the original (first) commit
- **Unchecked** = file moves to the new child commit
- All files checked by default (user unchecks files to move them out)

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`.

#### 3. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the first commit's description.

**Pre-population**: Original description of the source commit.

#### 4. Options (single row)
- **"Create parallel commits"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--parallel` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Split"** button (OK action) — executes the split
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be unchecked (otherwise no split happens — nothing moves to second commit)
- At least one file must be checked (otherwise original becomes empty — warn but allow)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SplitSpec(
    val revision: Revision,
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,   // Files for first commit (checked). Empty = all.
    val description: Description?,    // -m flag for first commit
    val parallel: Boolean             // --parallel flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun split(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    parallel: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun split(revision, filePaths, description, parallel) =
    execute(root, splitArgs(revision, filePaths, description, parallel))

internal fun splitArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    parallel: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("split")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (parallel) add("--parallel")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`splitAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun splitAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.split", AllIcons.Actions.SplitVertically) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SplitDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { split(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.parallel) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.split.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

**Note**: Unlike squash where we need to load files async then show dialog, split loads files synchronously because the action already runs from the nullAndDumbAwareAction context (which is on EDT after user clicks). To avoid blocking EDT, wrap file loading in a background task or use the two-phase async pattern from squash.

## Refresh After Split

Call `repo.invalidate(select = target.id)`:
- The original change retains its change ID with the "kept" files
- The new child commit gets a new change ID
- Select the original change so the user can see the result

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/splitAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/split/SplitDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:
- `CommandExecutor.kt` — Add `split()` method
- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `split()` + `splitArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add split to context menu
- `JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
```properties
log.action.split=Split...
log.action.split.tooltip=Split this change into two changes
log.action.split.error.title=Split Failed
log.action.split.error.message=Failed to split change:\n{0}
dialog.split.title=Split Change
dialog.split.source=Source
dialog.split.files=Files to keep in first commit
dialog.split.files.hint=Checked files stay in the original commit; unchecked files move to a new child
dialog.split.description=Description for first commit
dialog.split.parallel=Create parallel commits (siblings instead of parent/child)
dialog.split.validation.none.unchecked=Uncheck at least one file to split
dialog.split.button=Split
```

## Tests

- `CliExecutorSplitTest.kt` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations:
  - Basic split (revision only)
  - With file paths
  - With `-m` description
  - With `--parallel`
  - All combinations

- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt` — Add split availability tests:
  - Immutable entries filtered out (single-select, `!immutable`)
  - Mutable entries pass through
  - Working copy mutable entries pass through
  - Multi-select returns null (single-select only)

## Design Rationale

**Checked = stays, unchecked = moves**: This matches `jj split`'s CLI semantics where filesets specify what stays. Users think "I want to keep these files here and move the rest out."

**No description for second commit**: `jj split` handles the second commit's description automatically (uses the original description or prompts). Adding a second text area would complicate the UI for a rare need.

**Parallel option**: Simple checkbox because it's a single flag. Advanced users who want parallel commits can toggle it; most users leave it unchecked.

**Shared FileSelectionPanel**: Reuses the same component as squash, avoiding duplicate checkbox tree infrastructure."}

{"design":"# Squash Operation UI Design

## Overview

One context menu action — **"Squash into Parent..."** — that opens a dialog supporting both whole-change and selective-file squash. No need for separate subtasks; it's one cohesive feature with a single dialog.

The dialog follows the pattern established by the rebase dialog: action factory → dialog → command execution → refresh.

## Scope

**In scope (this issue)**:
- Squash a single mutable change into its parent: `jj squash -r <rev>`
- Squash selected files from a change into its parent: `jj squash -r <rev> <paths...>`
- Description editing for the combined result
- Option to keep the emptied source change

**Out of scope (future issues)**:
- `--from/--into` arbitrary source/destination squash (not just into parent)
- Multi-source squash (`--from A --from B --into C`)
- Interactive hunk-level selection (`--interactive`)

## Action Initiation

### Context Menu Entry
Add **"Squash into Parent..."** to the log table context menu, in the same separator group as Rebase (line 49 area of `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt`).

**Availability rules** (same pattern as abandon/edit):
- Single selection only (no multi-select — `jj squash` operates on one revision)
- Mutable changes only (`!entry.immutable`)
- Enabled for working copy too (squash @ into @-)

**Null-guard pattern**:
```kotlin
add(squashAction(project, entry?.takeIf { !it.immutable }))
```

## Dialog Design: SquashDialog

A `DialogWrapper` with vertical layout, simpler than RebaseDialog (no destination picker needed since destination is always the parent).

### Layout (top to bottom)

#### 1. Source Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the change being squashed — HTML-rendered like the rebase source panel:
- Change ID (colored) + description + decorations (bookmarks, working copy indicator)
- Author + timestamp

#### 2. Destination Panel (read-only, fixed height)
Shows the parent change (destination of squash) — same HTML rendering:
- "Into parent:" label
- Change ID + description + decorations

#### 3. Files Panel (expandable, main area)
Uses the shared `FileSelectionPanel` from jj-idea-7aha.

**File loading**: Call `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` to get the file list for the source change, then pass to `FileSelectionPanel.setChanges()`. This handles rename detection and all status types.

**Checkbox behavior** (provided by IntelliJ's `ChangesTree` infrastructure via `FileSelectionPanel`):
- All files checked by default (= whole squash)
- User unchecks files to exclude them (= selective squash)
- Directory nodes show three-state: all/some/none children selected
- Click on directory checkbox toggles all children
- Space key toggles selected node(s)

#### 4. Description Panel (editable, fixed height ~4 lines)
A multiline text area for the squashed result's description.

**Pre-population strategy**: Concatenate source and destination descriptions, separated by a blank line (matching `jj squash` default behavior):
```
<destination description>

<source description>
```

If the source description is empty (`(no description set)`), just show the destination description.

#### 5. Options (single row)
- **"Keep emptied source"** checkbox (unchecked by default) → maps to `--keep-emptied` flag

### Dialog Buttons
- **"Squash"** button (OK action) — executes the squash
- **Cancel** button

### Validation
- At least one file must be selected (disable "Squash" button otherwise)
- If all files are selected, omit file paths from command (simpler, whole squash)

### Data Structure
```kotlin
data class SquashSpec(
    val revision: Revision,        // Source change to squash
    val filePaths: List<FilePath>,  // Empty = all files (whole squash)
    val description: Description?,  // null = let jj merge descriptions; non-null = -m flag
    val keepEmptied: Boolean        // --keep-emptied flag
)
```

## Command Executor

### Interface addition (`CommandExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
fun squash(
    revision: Revision = WorkingCopy,
    filePaths: List<FilePath> = emptyList(),
    description: Description? = null,
    keepEmptied: Boolean = false
): CommandResult
```

### CLI implementation (`CliExecutor.kt`)
```kotlin
override fun squash(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied) =
    execute(root, squashArgs(revision, filePaths, description, keepEmptied))

internal fun squashArgs(
    revision: Revision,
    filePaths: List<FilePath>,
    description: Description?,
    keepEmptied: Boolean
): List<String> = buildList {
    add("squash")
    add("-r"); add(revision.toString())
    if (description != null) { add("-m"); add(description.actual) }
    if (keepEmptied) add("--keep-emptied")
    filePaths.forEach { add(it.relativeTo(root)) }
}
```

## Action Factory (`squashAction.kt`)

```kotlin
fun squashAction(project: Project, entry: LogEntry?) =
    nullAndDumbAwareAction(entry, "log.action.squash", <squash-icon>) {
        val repo = target.repo
        val changes = ChangeService.loadChanges(target)
        val dialog = SquashDialog(project, target, changes)
        if (!dialog.showAndGet()) return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        val spec = dialog.result ?: return@nullAndDumbAwareAction
        repo.commandExecutor
            .createCommand { squash(spec.revision, spec.filePaths, spec.description, spec.keepEmptied) }
            .onSuccess { repo.invalidate(select = target.id) }
            .onFailureTellUser("log.action.squash.error", project, log)
            .executeAsync()
    }
```

## Refresh After Squash

Call `repo.invalidate(select = ...)`:
- If `keepEmptied = false`: select the parent (since source is gone) — but actually, the change ID persists since it's the destination. Select the destination (parent) change.
- If `keepEmptied = true`: select the source change (it's still there, just empty now)

## File Structure

New files:
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/vcs/actions/squashAction.kt` — Action factory
- `src/main/kotlin/in/kkkev/jjidea/ui/squash/SquashDialog.kt` — Dialog

Modified files:
- `CommandExecutor.kt` — Add `squash()` method
- `CliExecutor.kt` — Implement `squash()` + `squashArgs()`
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActions.kt` — Add squash to context menu
- `JujutsuBundle.properties` — Add message keys

## Bundle Properties
```properties
log.action.squash=Squash into Parent...
log.action.squash.tooltip=Squash this change into its parent
log.action.squash.error.title=Squash Failed
log.action.squash.error.message=Failed to squash:\n{0}
dialog.squash.title=Squash into Parent
dialog.squash.source=Source
dialog.squash.destination=Into parent
dialog.squash.files=Files to squash
dialog.squash.files.none=Select at least one file
dialog.squash.description=Combined description
dialog.squash.keep.emptied=Keep emptied source change
dialog.squash.button=Squash
```

## Tests

- `CliExecutorSquashTest.kt` — Verify CLI arg construction for all flag combinations (whole, selective, with description, keep-emptied)
- `JujutsuLogContextMenuActionsTest.kt` — Add squash availability tests (immutable filtered out, single-select only, mutable allowed)

## Design Rationale

**Single action with file tree vs. two separate actions**: A single dialog with checkboxes (all checked by default) covers both whole and selective squash. Users who want whole squash just click "Squash" immediately. Users who want selective squash uncheck files. This matches IntelliJ's commit dialog pattern and avoids polluting the context menu with two similar entries.

**No destination picker**: Unlike rebase, squash destination is always the parent. Showing a destination picker would add complexity for a rare use case (`--from/--into`). That can be a separate "Squash Into..." action in a future issue.

**Description pre-population**: Concatenating descriptions matches `jj squash` default behavior. Users can edit before confirming. This avoids the jarring experience of jj opening $EDITOR.

**Checkbox tree reuse**: The shared `FileSelectionPanel` (jj-idea-7aha) wraps `JujutsuChangesTree` with `showCheckboxes = true`. The three-state checkbox behavior, directory toggling, and keyboard handling come for free from the platform.

**ChangeService.loadChanges**: Uses the existing `ChangeService.loadChanges(entry)` method which handles rename detection and all status types, rather than rolling a custom `diffSummary` parser."}

{"description":"The log reloads on every file change in the IDE because it subscribes to `repositoryStates`, which invalidates via BulkFileListener on a 300ms debounce. File edits don't change log history — only the working copy entry's empty/non-empty flag could change, and that only matters to the working copy panel.

After jj-idea-i4k5 decouples the log from `repositoryStates`, decide whether `logRefresh` should fire on file changes at all, or only on explicit VCS operations.","design":"## Problem

The log reloads on every file change because it subscribes to `repositoryStates`, which invalidates on a 300ms debounce from BulkFileListener. Editing a file in the IDE triggers a full log reload even though only the working copy entry could have changed.

## Analysis

After jj-idea-i4k5 is implemented, the log subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. This creates two options:

1. **Don't fire `logRefresh` from BulkFileListener at all** — file edits don't change log history, only the WC entry. The WC panel already handles this via `repositoryStates`. The log would only refresh on explicit VCS operations and the refresh button.

2. **Debounce `logRefresh` separately** with a longer interval (e.g., 1-2 seconds) for file-triggered refreshes vs immediate for VCS operations.

Option 1 is simpler and probably correct — the log shows commit history, not live file status. The WC entry in the log (empty/non-empty flag, description) only changes via VCS operations, not file edits.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 must land first to decouple log refresh from `repositoryStates`."}

{"design":"## Context

This bead is about the standard `VcsLogProvider.subscribeToRootRefreshEvents()` hook, which is for the built-in IntelliJ VCS log. Since we use a custom log panel (not IntelliJ's built-in log), this hook is less relevant — it would only matter if we keep the legacy log integration (jj-idea-v1o).

For the custom log, external JJ operations (CLI commands outside the IDE) should trigger a refresh. After jj-idea-i4k5, the `logRefresh` notifier is the right hook point.

## Design

**For external JJ operation detection**: Watch `.jj/repo/op_heads/` directory for changes. When op_heads change, an external operation has occurred. Fire `logRefresh.notify(Unit)` (debounced).

The BulkFileListener in `JujutsuStateModel` currently filters OUT `.jj` directory events via `isUnderJjDirectory()`. To detect external operations, add a separate check for `.jj/repo/op_heads/` changes:

```kotlin
// In BulkFileListener.after()
if (events.any { isOpHeadsChange(it) }) {
    // External JJ operation detected — debounce and refresh log
    logRefreshAlarm.cancelAllRequests()
    logRefreshAlarm.addRequest({ logRefresh.notify(Unit) }, 500)
}
```

**For the legacy VCS log hook** (`subscribeToRootRefreshEvents`): Same approach — watch op_heads and call `refresher.refresh(root)`. But this is only needed if the legacy log is kept.

## Depends on

jj-idea-i4k5 provides the `logRefresh` notifier to fire."}

{"design":"## Context

`repositoryStates` recomputes all working copies across all repos when any single repo changes. After jj-idea-i4k5, this concern is orthogonal to the log refresh path — `repositoryStates` only serves the working copy panel and dirty scope.

## Design (unchanged from original)

Key `repositoryStates` by repository so that invalidation is per-repo. Options:

1. Change to `NotifiableState<Map<JujutsuRepository, LogEntry>>` with per-repo invalidation
2. Keep current structure but only reload the changed repo's entry, merging with cached entries for other repos

Option 2 is simpler: the loader receives a hint about which repo changed, loads only that repo's `jj log -r @`, and merges with the cached set.

## Relationship to jj-idea-i4k5

Independent. `logRefresh` fires unconditionally (no per-repo optimization needed for the log signal). `repositoryStates` per-repo optimization only affects the WC panel and dirty scope paths."}

{"design":"## Root Cause

`abandonChangeAction` calls `repo.invalidate(select = WorkingCopy)`, which calls `repositoryStates.invalidate()`. The loader runs `jj log -r @` and compares with the previous value via `StateKey` equality. When abandoning a non-WC change, the working copy hasn't changed, so `NotifiableState` suppresses the notification. The log panel only subscribes to `repositoryStates`, so it never hears about the abandon → no refresh.

This is a symptom of the architectural mismatch described in jj-idea-i4k5: the log panel subscribes to a working-copy-state signal, but needs an unconditional "repo changed" signal.

## Fix

Implement jj-idea-i4k5 (add `logRefresh` notifier). The log panel subscribes to `logRefresh` instead of `repositoryStates`. Since `logRefresh` fires unconditionally from `repo.invalidate()`, the log always refreshes after abandon.

## Selection after abandon

Per comment: currently selects working copy even when abandoning a different change. After implementing this fix, the abandon action should select one of the abandoned change's parents instead of `WorkingCopy`. This requires the action to resolve parent IDs before abandoning (from the `LogEntry.parentIds`)."}
